page: Microsoft Azure F32als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Falsv6 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 processor. It features 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The hardware allocation provides 64.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to external high-performance storage volumes.

In benchmark evaluations, the F32als_v6 demonstrates strong compute capabilities. Single-core CPU performance via stress-ng and multi-core performance via Geekbench both rank in the top tier, placing in the top 10% of tested servers. Multi-core stress-ng performance, PassMark CPU scores, and Geekbench software compilation tests fall within the top 25%. Memory bandwidth tests show strong write performance for large blocks and cached read performance, though uncached large-block reads remain in the average tier. Cryptographic and vector instruction sets, including AVX-512 support, contribute to strong performance in PassMark encryption and physics tests.

The primary tradeoff of the F32als_v6 is its low memory density of 2.0 GB per vCPU and the complete absence of local storage. This configuration makes it highly cost-efficient for compute-bound workloads that do not require massive in-memory databases or local scratch disks. It is well-suited for tasks such as software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and text processing. Additionally, it shows strong performance in small-to-medium LLM inference tasks, particularly in text generation, making it a viable option for lightweight machine learning inference and high-throughput web serving.
description: The Microsoft Azure F32als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine from the Falsv6 family, featuring 32 dedicated physical cores on an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture, it is configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance and multi-core Geekbench scores, alongside strong performance in software compilation, ray tracing, and cryptography. The low memory density presents a tradeoff, making this server highly cost-efficient for compute-heavy, memory-light tasks. It is best suited for workloads such as software compilation, image processing, text processing, and small-to-medium LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F32als_v6 is a compute-optimized VM featuring 32 AMD EPYC cores, 64 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, ideal for compute-heavy workloads without high memory or local storage needs.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F32als_v6 is a compute-optimized VM with 32 AMD EPYC cores and 64 GB RAM, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual machine delivering high-frequency AMD EPYC performance for CPU-intensive workloads requiring minimal memory overhead.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.7 GHz AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 32 dedicated physical cores
Configured with 64.0 GB of RAM, offering a compute-focused 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and multi-core Geekbench scores
Lacks local storage and GPUs, but supports Azure Premium Storage
Optimized for software compilation, image processing, and text processing
Capable of efficient small-to-medium LLM inference and cryptographic tasks
